article_title: Perplexity Unveils Personal Computer: An Always-On AI Agent Running on Mac Mini
summary: Well-written article with two source attribution errors: CEO quote misattributed to VentureBeat (appears in Axios/Digital Trends instead), and SOC 2 Type II certification claim not supported by any cited source

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality
Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

CEO quote misattributed to VentureBeat. The Srinivas quote ('A traditional operating system takes instructions; an AI operating system takes objectives') does not appear in the VentureBeat article. VentureBeat quotes Dmitry Shevelenko, not Srinivas. The quote is found in the Axios and Digital Trends articles instead. The inline citation link should point to Axios or Digital Trends.

SOC 2 Type II certification claim is not supported by any cited source. The article states the enterprise package includes 'SOC 2 Type II certification' but none of the five sources mention SOC 2 Type II. VentureBeat mentions 'enterprise-grade audit trails' and Digital Trends mentions 'compliance features', but neither specifies SOC 2 Type II. This claim should be removed or replaced with what the sources actually state.

content_preview.title: Perplexity Unveils Personal Computer: An Always-On AI Agent Running on Mac Mini
content_preview.summary: Perplexity announces Personal Computer at Ask 2026, turning a Mac mini into a persistent AI agent with local file access, 20 orchestrated models, and enterprise integrations.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Perplexity used its inaugural Ask 2026 developer conference — held inside a former church in San Francisco's North Beach — to announce Personal Computer, a product that turns a Mac mini into an always-on AI agent with local access to a user's files, applications, and sessions. The announcement, [reported by 9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/11/perplexitys-personal-computer-is-a-cloud-based-ai-agent-running-on-mac-mini/), marks a significant expansion for the company, which also u...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article with clear sections covering the product announcement, capabilities, pricing, security, and enterprise offering. Word count (584) is appropriate for the News category. Writing is professional and informative.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources fetched and verified. 9to5Mac confirms core product details (Mac mini, always-on, local access, cloud processing, security model). Axios confirms CEO quote, conference venue, and competitive positioning vs OpenClaw. VentureBeat confirms enterprise features, Slack integration, connectors, and ~20 AI models. TechCrunch confirms late-February Perplexity Computer launch. Digital Trends confirms $200/month pricing, 10,000 credits, waitlist, and Mac-only launch. Two attribution errors found (see findings).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Most claims are well-sourced. Two errors: (1) The Srinivas quote is attributed to VentureBeat but actually appears in Axios and Digital Trends — VentureBeat quotes Shevelenko instead. (2) SOC 2 Type II certification is stated as fact but no cited source mentions it specifically.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately flags unanswered questions without editorializing. No sensationalism detected.
editor_notes.originality: First article on this topic — no duplicate or overlapping coverage found in recent publications.
concerns: CEO quote 'A traditional operating system takes instructions; an AI operating system takes objectives' is inline-cited to VentureBeat but does not appear in that article. It appears in Axios and Digital Trends.
SOC 2 Type II certification claim appears in the enterprise section but is not supported by any of the five cited sources.
recommendations: Change the inline citation for the Srinivas quote from VentureBeat to Axios or Digital Trends.

Remove 'SOC 2 Type II certification' from the enterprise feature list, or replace with sourced language such as 'enterprise-grade audit trails' (VentureBeat) or 'compliance features' (Digital Trends).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong submission on a timely topic with good structure and appropriate depth. The two source attribution issues are fixable and do not indicate systemic problems. Human-requested article received heightened scrutiny — no framing bias or independence concerns detected. Recommend REQUEST_CHANGES to correct the two attribution errors before publication.
